steps.title: Identify the opening modifier
steps.content: The sentence opens with the participial phrase Forming extensive networks via mycorrhizal association (the dashes set off the appositive that is, a symbiotic relationship between plants and fungi). The blank must begin with a subject that the participle Forming can logically modify.

steps.title: Apply the dangling/misplaced modifier rule
steps.content:
The grammar rule: an opening participial phrase must be followed by a subject that can logically perform the action of the participle. Here, the subject must be something that actually forms the networks.
  • A: subject is it — no clear referent that forms networks (dangling modifier)
  • B: subject is the transport of nutrients — transport doesn't form networks (dangling modifier)
  • C: subject is nutrients — nutrients don't form networks (dangling modifier)
  • D: subject is pine trees and the fungus Tricholoma matsutake — these can logically form the networks
That matches choice D — D is correct.
